Department of Corporate Services “Exchange Plaza” Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Bandra-Kurla Complex Dalal Street Bandra (East) Mumbai – 400 001 Mumbai – 400 001 Security Code No. 500380 Symbol: JKLAKSHMI, Series: EQ Through: BSE Listing Centre Through: NEAPS Dear Sir/ Madam, Update- Disclosure under Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, as amended - Declaration as Preferred Bidder in the e-Auction for Juipahar New Umrangso Limestone Block (A and B), Assam Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, as amended, we wish to inform you that the Company has participated in the e- auction conducted by the Government of Assam wherein the Company has been declared as the ‘Preferred Bidder’ for the Mining Lease for Juipahar New Umrangso Limestone Block (A and B), Assam. Yours faithfully, For JK Lakshmi Cement Limited (Amit Chaurasia) Company Secretary

Acquisitions & M&A.headline: KPI Green Energy Limited has informed the exchange regarding Press Release titled "KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio'. ....
Acquisitions & M&A.pdf_text: KPI/MAT/MAR/2026/757 Date: March 12, 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai - 400 001 Bandra (E), Mumbai - 400051 Scrip Code: 542323 Symbol: KPIGREEN Sub.: Press Release – KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are enclosing herewith a Press Release titled “KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio”. This portfolio highlights a robust and sustainable annuity revenue stream from long-term contracted projects under PPAs (many of them 25-year PPA’s), reinforcing the Company’s strategic transition toward an asset-backed IPP model complemented by its established EPC expertise. This is for your information and records. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For KPI Green Energy Limited Moh. Sohil Yusuf Dabhoya Whole Time Director DIN: 07112947For immediate release KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio KPI Green Energy Limited (“KPI Green” or “the Company”) has recently energised an additional 35 MWp of solar capacity, taking its total operational IPP capacity to 589 MWp. With projects currently in hand, the Company remains well on track to commission an additional 1,582 MWp in the near term, which will expand its total IPP portfolio to 2.17 GWp. This continued capacity addition further strengthens KPI Green’s position as a leading Independent Power Producer (IPP). This portfolio underscores a robust and sustainable annuity revenue stream from long-term contracted projects under Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), many with tenures of up to 25 years. It further reinforces the Company’s strategic transition toward an asset-backed Independent Power Produce

We request you to kindly take the aforesaid information on record. Thanking You Yours Faithfully, For Natural Capsules Limited Pranjal Deshmukh Company Secretary & Compliance Officer M.No: A66119P RE S S & M E D I A RE L E A S E Natural Biogenex and Fermbox Bio Enter Strategic Framework and Manufacturing Agreements to Strengthen Fermentation-Based CDMO Capabilities Natural Biogenex Private Limited, a subsidiary of Natural Capsules Limited, has executed definitive Framework and Contract Manufacturing Agreements with Fermbox Bio Private Limited to jointly advance fermentation-based Contract Development and Manufacturing (CDMO) services. Under this collaboration, Fermbox will provide advanced fermentation technology and equipment placement of up to ₹60 crore at NBPL’s state-of-the-art facility in Tumkur, while NBPL will undertake licensed manufacturing, quality assurance and regulatory compliance. The partnership positions the Company to serve global biotechnology and biopharma clients with integrated, GMP-compliant manufacturing capacity and enhanced process efficiency. J Towers, Exchange Plaza, C-1, Block G, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E), Mumbai – 400001 Mumbai – 400051 Scrip Code: 506642 Symbol: SADHNANIQ Sub: Outcome of the meeting of Rights Issue Committee of Sadhana Nitro Chem Limited held on March 12, 2026 Ref: Allotment of Equity Shares under Right Issue of Sadhana Nitro Chem Limited (“the Company”) Dear Sir/ Madam, The Empowered Rights Issue Committee (“RIC”), duly authorised by the Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held today, i.e., Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 7:00 p.m., has inter alia considered and approved the allotment of equity shares pursuant to the Rights Issue of the Company. In relation to the aforesaid Rights Issue and pursuant to the finalization of the Basis of Allotment of the Rights Issue in consultation with the Registrar to the Issue and BSE Limited, the Designated Stock Exchange , the RIC at its meeting held on March 12, 2026 has inter alia passed the resolution for approving the allotment of 263,52,83,328 fully paid up Equity Shares at an issue price of ₹ 1/- per Equity Share to the eligible equity shareholders or applicants (renouncees), as the case may be pursuant to the Rights Issue. Further, 5128 (Five Thousand One Hundred and Twenty Eight) Rights Equity Shares have been kept in abeyance pending regulatory/ other clearances, as may be applicable. Accordingly, the paid-up equity share capital of the Company has been increased from Rs. 32,94,11,057 to Rs. 2,96,46,94,385 by addition of 263,52,83,328 equity shares. The said fully paid-up Equity Shares allotted to eligible equity shareholders or applicants (renouncees), as the case may be, shall rank pari- passu in all respect with the existing fully paid-up Equity Shares of the Company. Dear Sir/Madam, This is to inform that Ten X Realty Limited (‘TXRL’), a material subsidiary of Raymond Realty Limited (‘RRL/the Company’) has received a Show Cause Notice (‘SCN’) under sub-section (1) of section 148A of the Income Tax Act, 1961 dated March 11, 2026 from the Office of The Assistant Commissioner of Income Tax Central Circle 8(1), Mumbai (‘IT Department’) with respect to survey action conducted on September 25, 2025 under section 133A of the Income- tax Act, 1961. Regulatory & BSE Queries.pdf_text: March 12, 2026 National Stock Exchange of India Limited Exchange Plaza, C-1 Block G Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E) Mumbai – 400051, India Symbol: BHARTIARTL/ AIRTELPP BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400001, India Scrip Code: 532454/ 890157 Sub: Press Release Dear Sir/ Ma’am, We are enclosing herewith a press release dated March 12, 2026 titled ‘Customer Day 2026: Airtel Reinforces its Culture of Customer Obsession’ being issued by the Company. Kindly take the same on record. Thanking you, Sincerely yours, For Bharti Airtel Limited Rohit Krishan Puri Company Secretary & Compliance Officer Bharti Airtel Limited (a Bharti Enterprise) Regd. Office: Airtel Center, Plot No. 16, Udyog Vihar, Phase-IV, Gurugram - 122015, India Corporate Office: Bharti Crescent, 1, Nelson Mandela Road, Vasant Kunj, Phase II, New Delhi - 110070, India T.: +91-124-4222222, F.: +91-124-4248063, Email: compliance.officer@bharti.in, Website: www.airtel.in CIN: L74899HR1995PLC095967Customer Day 2026: Airtel Reinforces its Culture of Customer Obsession A renewed focus on raising its delivered experience and solving for customer problems at scale New Delhi, 12 March 2026: Airtel today marked Customer Day 2026, a mass movement within the organization focused on understanding customer pain points and continuously improving its delivered experiences. Nearly twenty thousand Airtel employees—from leadership teams to functional specialists—stepped out of their offices and into the field, working alongside frontline teams to engage directly with customers, shadowing field engineers, visiting homes and shops, and interacting at retail and service centres to gain an unfiltered view of the issues that matter most. Customer Day at Airtel serves as a deliberate pause for the organisation to introspect and ask a simple but critical question: what more can we do for our customers? Commenting on the initiative, Shashwat Sharma, Managing Director and CEO, Airtel India, said: “Customer

The company also introduced Battery-As-A-Service (BaaS) across its EV portfolio, offering customers a new and flexible ownership model. Customers who prefers the TVS Orbiter V1 without BaaS, can purchase it at ₹84,500 (ex-showroom Delhi, inclusive of PM e-Drive). The PPAs has been signed for a tenor of 25 years on 2 March 2026. Vasudevan has been removed from the list of proposed allottees under Item No. 1 regarding the preferential issue of equity shares. All other resolutions, including the issuance of sweat equity shares to Directors (Item No. 2), the acquisition of equity stake in M/s Beontyme Technologies Private Limited (Item No. 3), preferential issue of equity stake in M/s Tulsea Media Private Limited (Item No. 4), and the increase in authorized share capital (Item No. 5), remain unchanged and as stated in the original Notice.  Integration: This Corrigendum should be read as an integral part of the EGM Notice dated March 02, 2026.  E-Voting: The remote e-voting period remains from Wednesday, March 25, 2026 (09:00 A.M.) to Friday, March 27, 2026 (05:00 P.M.). Management Changes.pdf_text: To, BSE Limited, Floor 25, P J Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400001 Script Code: 543963 Ref: Intimation with regards to “Shelter Pharma” secured order from First Vet Veterinary Medicines Trading LLC, UAE pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulation, 2015 Pursuant to regulation 30(4) of the SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulation, 2015, we are pleased to inform you that “Shelter Pharma Limited” secured a noteworthy order from First Vet Veterinary Medicines Trading LLC, UAE for Supply of veterinary / animal healthcare products total order worth 18,300 $. The details of the order as per SEBI is been annexed herewith this letter. Kindly take the note of above information on record and oblige. About Shelter Pharma Limited: Shelter Pharma Limited is a leading pharmaceutical and nutraceutical company known for its unwavering dedication to research, innovation, and quality. Founded with a vision to provide accessible and effective healthcare solutions, we have consistently delivered products that adhere to the highest industry standards. Our state-of-the-art facilities, world-class team of experts, and commitment to ethical practices have made us a trusted name in the industry. At Shelter Pharma Limited, our mission is to promote well-being and health through innovative, high-quality nutraceuticals. We take immense pride in being recognized for our commitment to producing safe and effective products that enhance the quality of life for individuals across the globe. Our passion for innovation drives us to develop nutraceutical products that empower individuals to lead healthier lives. We are guided by a strong code of ethics and a deep sense of responsibility to society. For any inquiries or additional information, please feel free to contact our Investor Relations team. Kindly take the above on record and oblige. Thanking You. FOR, SHELTER PHARMA LIMITED ___________________________________ MUSTAQIM N

Management Changes.pdf_text: Ref: GSL/CS/SE/2025-26 Date: 12.03.2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange Limited Department of Corporate Services, Exchange Plaza, 5th Floor, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Plot No. C/1, G Block, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400001 Bandra-Kurla Complex, Mumbai – 400051 Script code: 544140 Symbol: GOPAL Sub: Disclosure under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 – Business Updates Dear Sir / Madam, Pursuant to the requirements of Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, as amended from time to time, we hereby submit a business update, further details are attached herewith. Yours Faithfully, For, GOPAL SNACKS LIMITED CS Mayur Gangani Head — Legal & Compliance cum Company Secretary Membership No. F9980 Encls: a/aBusiness Updates Rajkot, 12th March 2026: Gopal Snacks Limited, (“Gopal Snacks Ltd” or the “Company”) (BSE: 544140; NSE: GOPAL) a leading FMCG company in India renowned for its high- quality snack products, has announced a business update regarding the ongoing gas supply shortage. Recently Government of India has issued Notification on restriction of usage of Gas for Commercial use due to tensions in Middle East, Prioritizing the same for Domestic use. This is to clarify that, Company is already using Bio coal at its Modasa and Nagpur Facilities and its manufacturing facilities are running smoothly and will be able to serve its customers demand without any interruption. Gopal Snacks has always been looking to focus on taking strategic steps to use alternative sources of fuel, making it more cost effective and reducing the dependency on single source of fuel to overcome any such unforeseen challenges. The Modasa and Nagpur plants, both crucial to the company's manufacturing operations, are running normally and are well-equipped to meet the current and future production demands, ensuring that there will be no delays in product d

Management Changes.pdf_text: केंद्रीय कायाालय INVESTORS RELATION DIVISION Central Office CO:IRD:2025:26:270 Date: 12.03.2026 National Stock Exchange of India Limited BSE Limited Exchange Plaza, Corporate Relationship Dept., Plot No. C/1, ‘G’ Block, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Dalal Street, Fort, Bandra (E), Mumbai-400 051 Mumbai-400001 Scrip code – CENTRALBK Scrip Code – 532885 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub: Intimation under SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 – Distribution Agreement with M/s HDFC Asset Management Company Limited for the distribution of Mutual Fund Products. We would like to inform that Central Bank of India has entered into distributorship agreement with M/s. HDFC Asset Management Company Limited for distribution of their Mutual Fund Products to its customers. The participation by both the entities in this arrangement will result in offering the customers of Central Bank of India, an opportunity to unlock the value of their savings through various mutual fund products offered by M/s HDFC Asset Management Company Limited. M/s. HDFC Asset Management Company Limited (HDFC AMC) was established in the year 1999. HDFC AMC is one of the leading asset management Company in India, serving around above 1.5 crores unique investors with total asset under management (AUM) of Rs.9.21 trillion as on 31st December, 2025 under its suite of investment offerings. Please take the above on your record. Thanking you. Yours faithfully, For CENTRAL BANK OF INDIA MUKUL N. DANDIGE Chief Financial Officer केंद्रीय कायाालय: चंदर मुखी, नरीमन पॉइंट, मुंबई - 400 021 दूरभाष/Tel.: 022-6638 7575 Central Office: Chander Mukhi, Nariman Point, Mumbai - 400 021 ईमेल/Email ID: smird@centralbank.bank.in www.centralbank.bank.in

Management Changes.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, Listing Department Listing Department BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Bandra Kurla Complex Dalal Street, Fort Bandra East Mumbai-400 001 Mumbai – 400 051 NSE Symbol: AURUM BSE Scrip Code: 539289 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub.: Outcome of Board Meeting held on March 12, 2026 and disclosure under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations 2015, as amended (“Listing Regulations”). Pursuant to Regulations 30 and other applicable provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“Listing Regulations”), and in furtherance of the resolution passed by the shareholders of the Company on September 23, 2025, we wish to inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held on March 12, 2026, inter alia, considered and approved the sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 situated at MIDC, MBP-1, Plot Nos. P-136 & 136/1, TTC Industrial Area, Mahape, Navi Mumbai for a consideration of approximately ₹ 112 Crores (Rupees One Hundred and Twelve Crore Only). The brief details as required under Regulation 30 of the Listing Regulations read with the SEBI Master Circular HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 updated on January 30, 2026 is enclosed herewith as Annexure I. A Press Release being issued by the Company in this regard is also enclosed herewith as Annexure II. The meeting of the Board of Directors commenced at 2.00 p.m. and concluded at 2.18 p.m. You are requested to take the above on record. Thanking you. For Aurum PropTech Limited Sonia Jain Company Secretary & Compliance Officer

Board Decisions.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalai Street, Mumbai-400 011 BSE SCRIP SYMBOL: KATIPATANG; BSE SCRIP CODE: 531126 Subject: Outcome of Board Meeting held on March 12, 2026 Dear Sir/ Ma’am, In accordance with Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“SEBI Listing Regulations”) and in reference to the outcome of Board meeting held on March 03, 2026, we would like to inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company at their meeting held today i.e., Thursday, 12th March, 2026 at the Corporate Office of the Company at 504, Savitri Cinema Complex, Greater Kailash–II, New Delhi-110 048, has inter-alia, considered and approved the following businesses: 1. Preferential Issue of upto 14,58,333 Equity Shares of face value of Rs. 10/- each at an issue price of Rs. 24/- each, being the price determined in accordance with SEBI ICDR Regulations, 2018, to be issued to shareholders of Agnetta International Private Limited towards acquisition of 100% stake in Agnetta International Private Limited through share swap arrangement for an aggregate investment value of Rs. 3,50,00,000/- (“Share Swap 1”). 2. Preferential Issue of upto 3,87,280 Equity Shares of face value of Rs. 10/- each at an issue price of Rs. 24/- each, being the price determined in accordance with SEBI ICDR Regulations, 2018, to be issued to shareholders of Empyrean Spirits Private Limited (ESPL) towards acquisition of remaining 2% stake (38780 equity shares) in ESPL through share swap arrangement for an aggregate investment value of Rs. 92,94,720/- (“Share Swap 2”). 3. Approved the Draft Notice of Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) of the Company to be held on Monday, April 06, 2026 & requisite authorization thereof. The Board meeting commenced at 01:00 pm (IST) & conclude at 02:15 pm (IST). Details as required under the Regulation 30 of SEBI Listing Regulations read with relevant circulars is attached as ‘Annexure A’ KATI PATANG LI

Addition in the main object i.e. Clause III [A] of the Memorandum of Association of the Company. The information required under the Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 read with SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/CFD/PoD2/CIR/P/0155 dated 11th November, 2024 is as follows: The Company targets to add the green energy business as an additional business vertical. To facilitate it, alteration of Memorandum of Association by amending the Clause III (Main Object Clause) of the Memorandum of Association (“the MOA”) of the Company by addition of the Main object no. 5 of the Clause III [A] is approved. 2. Appointment of Mr. Shalin Harshadbhai Patel (DIN: 08214933) as an Additional Non- Executive, Independent Director of the Company: Based on the recommendation of the Nomination and Remuneration Committee and further subject to the approval of Shareholders of the Company, the Board of Directors had appointed Mr. Shalin Harshadbhai Patel (DIN: 08214933) as an Additional Director (Non-Executive and Independent Director) of the Company and is recommended to be appointed as the Independent Director, not be liable to retire by rotation. The contents of the press release give full details. Please bring the aforesaid news to the notice of the members of the exchange and the investors’ at large. Yours faithfully, For, Zydus Lifesciences Limited Dhaval N. Soni Company Secretary and Compliance Officer Membership No. FCS7063 Encl.: As aboveZydus expands companion diagnostics portfolio with the launch of AI-powered Continuous Glucose Monitor DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM are designed for diabetic, Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD), and post- transplant patients who require continuous glycaemic surveillance and integrated remote care The next-gen CGM is paired with AI-powered analytics and a dedicated clinician remote- monitoring dashboard which is integrated with GoodFlipTM app designed by TatvaCareTM Ahmedabad, 12 March, 2026 Zydus Lifesciences Limited (“Zydus”), an innovation-led global life sciences company, today announced its new offering companion diagnostics portfolio. The company will be launching DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM, next-generation Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) devices that combine artificial intelligence- powered insights, and integrated remote care capabilities. The CGM system is designed to monitor glucose with an integrated AI layer to provide analytics and enable a closed-loop care ecosystem connecting patients, caregivers, and clinicians in real time. Scrip Code: 500470 Symbol: TATASTEEL Dear Madam, Sirs, Sub: Disclosure under Regulations 30 and 51 of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 This has reference to our earlier disclosures dated June 29, 2025 and December 19, 2025. Background: Tata Steel Limited (the ‘Company’) had earlier informed that it has received a show cause cum demand notice (‘SCN’) dated June 27, 2025 from the Office of the Commissioner (Audit), Central Tax, Ranchi, proposing disallowance of Input Tax Credit in contravention of the provisions of Section 74(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017/State Goods and Services Act, 2017 read with Section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, for the period FY2018-19 through FY2022-23. The SCN required the Company to show cause before the Additional/Joint Commissioner of Central GST & Central Excise, Jamshedpur, Jharkhand as to why the Goods and Service Tax (‘GST’) amounting to ₹1007,54,83,342/- along with interest/penalty, if any, shall not be demanded and recovered from the Company. Of the aggregate GST amount demanded, the Company had already paid GST of ₹514,19,36,211/- in the normal course of business and therefore the alleged GST exposure was of ₹493,35,47,131/- only. Further, the Company made its detailed submissions before the appropriate forum within the given timelines as per the said SCN. Analyst / Investor Meets.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, Department of Corporate Services, BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street Mumbai — 400 001. Scrip Code: 544296 ISIN: INE0DQN01013 Subject: Volume Movement Letter Ref: L/SURV/ONL/PV/APJ/ 2025-2026 / 3417 vide your Email dated March 10, 2026 With reference to the email received from BSE Limited, dated March 10, 2026 at 06:06 p.m. seeking clarification on the increase in volume of the Company’s security, we wish to inform you that the Company has been consistently making all requisite disclosures to the Stock Exchanges in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“SEBI LODR Regulations”), and will continue to adhere to the said Regulations. In relation to the captioned subject, we hereby clarify that there is no unpublished price sensitive information or event that has become due for disclosure under Regulation 30 of the SEBI LODR Regulations. The Company affirms that any movement in the volume of its shares is purely market-driven. Further, the management has provided an update dated 5 March 2026 regarding the impact of the current geopolitical tensions. The Company recognizes that periods of geopolitical uncertainty may influence market sentiment and contribute to short-term volatility. However, such developments do not affect the underlying fundamentals of the Company’s business. The above information shall also be made available on the website of the Company at https://nisusfin.com/investor- relations. Kindly acknowledge and take the above information on records. Thanking You. Yours faithfully For Nisus Finance Services Co Limited ________________ Amit Anil Goenka Managing Director DIN: 02778565

Pursuant to Regulations 30 and other applicable provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“Listing Regulations”), and in furtherance of the resolution passed by the shareholders of the Company on September 23, 2025, we wish to inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held on March 12, 2026, inter alia, considered and approved the sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 situated at MIDC, MBP-1, Plot Nos. P-136 & 136/1, TTC Industrial Area, Mahape, Navi Mumbai for a consideration of approximately ₹ 112 Crores (Rupees One Hundred and Twelve Crore Only). For Aurum PropTech Limited Sonia Jain Company Secretary & Compliance Officer

For Aurum PropTech Limited Sonia Jain Company Secretary & Compliance OfficerMEDIA RELEASE Aurum PropTech Exits non-core Assets for INR 112 Crores, Builds War Chest to Scale AI led PropTech Mumbai, India – March 12, 2026: Aurum PropTech Limited today announced that its Board of Directors has approved the sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 at Millennium Business Park, Navi Mumbai, for a total consideration of INR 112 Crores, approximately 15% above the valuation provided by the valuers. The transaction forms part of Aurum PropTech’s broader strategic shift toward AI-driven PropTech platforms, with the company committing significant capital toward building advanced artificial intelligence capabilities across the real estate ecosystem. The proceeds from the transaction will further strengthen Aurum’s growing AI investment pool and support the accelerated development of intelligent digital infrastructure for the real estate sector. Press Releases.pdf_text: Date: 12-03-2026 BSE Limited Listing Department Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Tower Dalal Street, Mumbai-400001 Scrip Code: 544646 Dear Sir/Madam, Subject: Intimation under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. In terms of Regulation 30 read with Schedule III of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are pleased to inform you that, Shipwaves Online Limited (the Company) has received a Purchase Order worth of Rs. 1,56,70,678/- (Rupees One Crore Fifty-Six Lakh Seventy Thousand Six Hundred Seventy-Eight only) from Hindalco Industries Limited for implementation of a comprehensive, end-to-end logistics digitalization solution tailored to Hindalco’s operational and compliance requirements. This engagement marks a significant milestone for Shipwaves, as Hindalco becomes the second company from the Aditya Birla Group ecosystem to adopt the Shipwaves platform, following the earlier onboarding of Grasim Industries. Hindalco selected Shipwaves after evaluating multiple technology platforms and recognizing the value of Shipwaves’ differentiated capabilities in digitizing and optimizing global freight operations. The platform will support Hindalco with: • End-to-End Export Shipment Management – enabling centralized planning, documentation, tracking, and execution of export shipments. • AI-Enabled Freight Bill Audit – automated validation of freight invoices to improve cost accuracy and reduce revenue leakage. • Digital Freight Tender Management for Imports – a structured and transparent platform for managing freight procurement and tendering across logistics partners. The adoption of the Shipwaves platform by Hindalco further validates the scalability, reliability, and enterprise-grade capabilities of Shipwaves’ technology for large global manufacturers.This milestone strengthens Shipwaves’ presence within the Aditya Birla Group ecosystem and demonstrates the growing adoption of digital suppl

Manikantha Company Secretary Membership No: A21918PRESS RELEASE Infosys Announces Expansion of Mohali Development Center Infosys strengthens its regional footprint with a new 350,000-sq. ft. integrated development campus in Mohali Bengaluru, India – March 12, 2026: Infosys (NSE, BSE, NYSE: INFY), a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting, today announced the expansion of its Mohali Development Center (DC) with a formal groundbreaking ceremony held at the campus, further strengthening its presence in North India. With this expansion, Infosys reaffirms its commitment to driving innovation, fostering local talent, and contributing to the economic and technological growth of Punjab. The ceremony was graced by Hon’ble Chief Minister of Punjab, S. Bhagwant Singh Mann and Hon’ble Cabinet Minister for Investment Promotion, Power and Local Government for Punjab, Shri. Sanjeev Arora, in the presence of Infosys leadership represented by Mr. Jayesh Sanghrajka, Chief Financial Officer, along with Mr. Sunil Kumar Dhareshwar, Executive Vice President, Infosys, Mr. Niladri Prasad Mishra, Senior Vice President, Infosys, and other senior officials from the State Government and Infosys. As part of this expansion, Infosys will develop a new Software Development Block along with supporting facilities. The expansion is planned to have a built-up area of approximately 350,000 square feet and will seat about 3,000 employees, strengthening Infosys’ ability to design, co-create, and deploy AI-led solutions at enterprise scale. Press Releases.pdf_text: केंद्रीय कायाालय INVESTORS RELATION DIVISION Central Office CO:IRD:2025:26:269 Date: 12.03.2026 National Stock Exchange of India Limited BSE Limited Exchange Plaza, Corporate Relationship Dept., Plot No. C/1, ‘G’ Block, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Dalal Street, Fort, Bandra (E), Mumbai-400 051 Mumbai-400001 Scrip code – CENTRALBK Scrip Code – 532885 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub: Intimation under SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 – Co-Lending Partnership with M/s. IIFL Finance Limited. We would like to inform that Central Bank of India has entered into Co-Lending Partnership with M/s. IIFL Finance Limited to offer Loans at competitive rates, subject to compliance with the applicable law(s) including the revised Co-Lending Arrangements (CLA) guidelines dated 28.11.2025 issued by Reserve Bank of India (RBI). The participation by both the entities in this Co-Lending arrangement will result in greater expansion of portfolio by Central Bank of India and M/s. IIFL Finance Limited. M/s. IIFL Finance Limited is a retail-focused non-banking financial company (NBFC) registered with the Reserve Bank of India. The NBFC is a leading player in the gold loan business across India. The Mumbai headquartered IIFL Finance Ltd. has a wide network of 4,761 branches and a wide customer base, mostly from the underserved segments. As on December 31, 2025, IIFL Finance Limited reported assets under management (AUM) of Rs.49,027.00 crores. Under the arrangement, M/s. IIFL Finance Limited will originate loan proposals, which will be processed by both lenders as per jointly formulated credit parameters and eligibility criteria. The borrower will benefit from the blended interest rate charged. M/s. IIFL Finance Limited will service the loan account throughout the life cycle of the loan. Operations & Capacity.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, To, BSE Limited, National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai- 400001 Bandra (E), Mumbai- 400001 Scrip Code: 543714 Symbol: LANDMARK Sub.: Press Release – Landmark Cars Enhances Its Hyderabad Aftersales Network with a New Mahindra & Mahindra Workshop Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are enclosing herewith a Press Release titled “Landmark Cars Enhances Its Hyderabad Aftersales Network with a New Mahindra & Mahindra Workshop”. Request you to please take the same on your record. Thanking You, Yours faithfully, For Landmark Cars Limited Amol Arvind Raje Company Secretary & Compliance Officer Mem. No.: A19459 Encl. as above Landmark Cars Limited CIN: L50100GJ2006PLC058553 | GSTIN: 24AABCL1862B1Z2 Registered Office: Landmark House, Opp. AEC, Near Gurudwara, S. G. Highway, Thaltej, Ahmedabad – 380059 Tel.: +91-7966185555 | Email: info@landmarkcars.in | Website: www.grouplandmark.inLandmark Cars Enhances Its Hyderabad Aftersales Network with a New Mahindra & Mahindra Workshop Thursday, 12th March 2026, Mumbai - Landmark Cars Limited (BSE: 543714 | NSE: LANDMARK), a leading premium automotive retail network in India, is set to expand its footprint in Hyderabad by opening a new Mahindra & Mahindra (‘M&M’) workshop in Kompally, Hyderabad. This workshop will help Landmark in catering to high demand witnessed in the existing two M&M workshops in Hyderabad. This workshop will be established in one of the wholly owned subsidiaries of Landmark Cars, namely, Landmark Mobility Private Ltd. This addition will take the total outlet count of Mahindra & Mahindra outlets to 8 in Hyderabad and 12 overall. This expansion is in line with Landmark’s strategy of deepening its engagement within each brand and geography. Such focused growth builds a robust network impact over time and continues to e

Transfer of Shareholding: Veranda Administrative Learning Solutions Private Limited, a wholly- owned subsidiary of Veranda Learning Solutions Limited (the Company), has transferred 100% of its shareholding held in Neyyar Academy Private Limited and Neyyar Education Private Limited (step-down wholly-owned subsidiaries of the Company), to Veranda Race Learning Solutions Private Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company, on March 11, 2026. 2. Merger Approval: The Board of Directors of the concerned entities on March 11, 2026, have approved the merger of: a) Veranda IAS Learning Solutions Private Limited (“Transferor Company-1”), wholly owned subsidiary of the Company, b) Neyyar Academy Private Limited (“Transferor Company-2”), step-down wholly owned subsidiary of the Company, with c) Veranda Race Learning Solutions Private Limited (“Transferee Company”), wholly owned subsidiary of the Company. The above restructuring has been undertaken as part of the Company’s strategic initiative to reorganise and consolidate its Government Test Preparation segment, with the objective of improving operational efficiencies and enabling focused growth of the segment. The details as required under Regulation 30 of the SEBI Listing Regulations, read with SEBI Circular No. HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFDPOD2/I/3762/2026 dated January 30, 2026, are provided as: • Annexure A – pertaining to Transf

Other Disclosures.headline: Press Release dated 12.03.2026
Other Disclosures.pdf_text: PRESS RELEASE CAMIKARA BECOMES THE WORLD’S BEST INDIAN RUM. BEATS GLOBAL RUM BRANDS After Indian single malt whisky captured global attention, Indian rum is now stepping into the spotlight — with Camikara leading the charge th 12 March 2026, New Delhi - Over the past decade, Indian single malt whisky has transformed the country’s reputation in global spirits, challenging long-established whisky regions and earning international acclaim. Now, a similar shift may be unfolding in another category deeply rooted in India’s agricultural heritage — Rum. In a landmark moment for Indian spirits, Camikara — India’s first pure cane juice aged rum — has secured top honours at two of the world’s most respected rum competitions, signalling the emergence of India as a serious contender in premium rum. At the Global Rum & Cachaça Masters Awards 2026 in the United Kingdom, Camikara 8-Year- Old was awarded the prestigious ‘Master Medal’, the competition’s highest distinction. In the same competition, Camikara 3-Year-Old secured its second consecutive ‘Gold Medal’, making Camikarathe only Indian rum to win both Master and Gold in the same year. Recognition soon followed in the United States. At the Aged Rum category tasting conducted by The Fifty Best, Camikara 8YO received a ‘Double Gold’ Medal, a distinction reserved for spirits that receive unanimous top scores from all judges. In the International blend category, Camikara 3YO also secured ‘Gold’, reinforcing the brand’s consistency across both expressions and international tasting panels. Piccadily Agro Industries Ltd. summaries.Fund Raise / QIP / NCD:
## Fund Raise / QIP / NCD

---

- **Manorama Industries Ltd (541974)**  — The board met on March 12, 2026 and approved a plan to raise money by selling shares to big institutional investors through a QIP (Qualified Institutional Placement).
  📊 **Impact: Neutral to Mildly Negative** — When a company sells new shares, existing shareholders own a slightly smaller piece of the same pie, like cutting a cake into more slices. The real impact depends on how much money is raised and what it's used for — details are not yet disclosed.
  👀 **Watch:** The QIP price per share when announced — if it's at a discount to the current market price, that's a red flag for existing shareholders.

---

- **Artificial Electronics Intelligent Material Ltd (526443)** — The company is converting warrants into fully paid-up equity shares via a preferential issue, following an in-principle application made on June 19, 2025.
  📊 **Impact: Neutral to Mildly Negative** — Warrant conversion means new shares are being created and handed to specific investors — this dilutes (reduces) the value of shares already held by regular investors, similar to adding more people to split the same prize money.
  👀 **Watch:** The exact number of new shares being created and the price at which warrants are being converted, once the final allotment is disclosed.

---

- **Star Housing Finance Ltd (539017)** — The company has issued a notice regarding a **Put Option** on its Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), meaning NCD holders may have the right to demand early repayment.
  📊 **Impact: Neutral to Mildly Negative** — If many NCD holders exercise this option and ask for their money back early, the company may face sudden cash pressure — like a landlord suddenly having to return multiple security deposits at the same time.
  👀 **Watch:** Whether the company announces sufficient cash reserves or a refinancing plan to meet potential early redemption demands from NCD holders.

---

- **Purple Finance Ltd (544191)** — The company has disclosed that it has made an **interest payment on its NCDs** (bonds it had issued to borrow money).
  📊 **Impact: Neutral** — Paying interest on time is a basic obligation, like paying your EMI on the due date. This filing simply confirms the company did what it was supposed to do — no surprise here.
  👀 **Watch:** Whether all future NCD interest payment dates are met on time, as any missed payment would be a serious warning sign for the company's financial health.

---

- **JK Lakshmi Cement Ltd (JKLAKSHMI / 500380)** — Despite the misleading headline, the actual filing reveals that JK Lakshmi Cement has been **declared the Preferred Bidder in an e-Auction for Juipahar New Umrangso** (likely a coal or limestone block), filed under Regulation 30 on March 12, 2026.
  📊 **Impact: Positive** — Winning a raw material block auction means the company could secure cheaper, self-sourced inputs for cement production — like a bakery buying its own wheat farm instead of buying wheat at market prices every month.
  👀 **Watch:** The official terms of the block allocation — specifically the quantity of reserves available and when the company can begin extraction, as that determines how much cost savings actually materialise.

---

- **
summaries.Acquisitions & M&A:
## Acquisitions & M&A — Pre-Market Briefing

---

- **Western Ministil Ltd (504998)** — The company issued a corrigendum (correction notice) to its earlier board meeting outcome dated 12th March 2026 regarding an acquisition announcement; specific corrected details were not disclosed in the headline.
  📊 **Impact:** Neutral — A correction to a previous announcement usually just fixes a paperwork error; until we see what changed, this doesn't move the needle either way.
  👀 **Watch:** What specific detail was corrected in the original acquisition announcement — the change could matter if it affects deal size or terms.

---

- **Gravita India Ltd (533282)** — Gravita India has signed definitive agreements to acquire Rashtriya Metal Industries Limited, though specific deal value was not disclosed in the filing.
  📊 **Impact:** Positive — Gravita is in the recycling and metal recovery business, so buying another metal company means it gets bigger, processes more material, and can earn more money.
  👀 **Watch:** The acquisition price and whether Rashtriya Metal brings any debt along with it, as that would affect how much the deal actually benefits Gravita shareholders.

---

- **JK Lakshmi Cement Ltd (500380)** — Filed a disclosure under Regulation 30 of SEBI LODR; specific details of the acquisition or transaction were not revealed in the headline alone.
  📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This appears to be a routine regulatory disclosure filing; without knowing the actual content, there is no clear positive or negative signal yet.
  👀 **Watch:** The actual content of the Regulation 30 disclosure once the full document is available, to determine if a material transaction is involved.

---

- **Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd (532898)** — Power Grid acquired NES Pune East New Transmission Limited under the Tariff-Based Competitive Bidding (TBCB) route, adding a new transmission project in the Pune East corridor.
  📊 **Impact:** Positive — Power Grid keeps winning new transmission projects, which means steady, government-backed income for years to come — like adding a new toll road to a collection that keeps paying rent.
  👀 **Watch:** The project commissioning timeline for NES Pune East, since delays in transmission projects can push back when revenue actually starts flowing in.

---

- **Oxford Industries Ltd (514414)** — A Public Announcement has been submitted to BSE under Regulations 3(1), 4, and 15(1) of the Takeover Code, indicating an open offer is being made to acquire shares from public shareholders; specific offer price and size not mentioned in the headline.
  📊 **Impact:** Positive — An open offer usually means someone wants to buy more shares of the company, often at a price higher than the market price, which is good news for existing shareholders.
  👀 **Watch:** The open offer price per share once the full public announcement document is published — if it is significantly above market price, expect a sharp stock rally.

---

- **AGI Infra Ltd (539042)** — Filed a disclosure under Regulation 30 of SEBI LODR; no specific acquisition details, deal size, or counterparty were mentioned in the headline.
  📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Routine compliance filing with no numbers or material details available; impossible to assess impact until the actual content is known.
  👀 **Watch:** The full text of the Regulation 30 filing
summaries.Regulatory & BSE Queries:
• **Accel Ltd (517494)** — Received a copy of an order passed by the Hon'ble NCLT, Chennai on 10 March 2026 (full details of the order not disclosed in the headline).
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — A court order from NCLT could matter a lot or very little depending on what it says, but since the actual content isn't shared here, we cannot call it good or bad news yet.
👀 **Watch:** What the NCLT Chennai order actually directs the company to do — whether it involves debt restructuring, winding up, or a merger will determine the real impact.

---

• **Fedders Holding Ltd (511628)** — Received an Adjudication Order from SEBI dated 11 March 2026 (penalty amount and specific violation not disclosed in the headline).
📊 **Impact:** Negative — When SEBI sends an adjudication order, it usually means the company or its officials are being fined or penalised for breaking stock market rules. That's never a good sign.
👀 **Watch:** The exact penalty amount and the nature of the SEBI violation, as repeated regulatory action can make investors lose confidence in the company's management.
